VAL-NOVA-003.evidence: POST /api/audits returns 202 with audit object (id, status=queued, mode=live, events). Code: NovaActAuditProvider.run_audit() orchestrates scenarios via _run_scenario_with_personas(). provider_registry.py: get_browser_provider() selects NovaActAuditProvider when effective_mode=='live' and NOVA_ACT_AVAILABLE. Readiness endpoint confirms browser_provider='NovaActAuditProvider'.
VAL-NOVA-003.details: Created audit c10a9583-e82a-4be4-8473-d64ab47d0504 via POST. Response: 202, structure includes id, target_url, mode, status, selected_scenarios, selected_personas, events array with queue phase event. run_audit() method (line 175) validates scenarios/personas against taxonomy, iterates each scenario calling _run_scenario_with_personas() which dispatches to individual scenario methods via _run_single_persona().

VAL-NOVA-004.evidence: Code: _run_cookie_consent_scenario() (line 337) navigates site, detects cookie banners via act_get() with BOOL_SCHEMA, then extracts structured CookieConsentObservation including: banner_present, accept/reject button text, reject_button_visible, accept/reject clicks_required, asymmetry_detected, deceptive_language, pre_selected_options, has_essential_only_option. Different persona paths implemented (privacy_sensitive rejects, cost_sensitive checks price-linked consent, exit_intent dismisses). Backend test: test_cookie_consent_returns_observation verifies observation structure.
VAL-NOVA-004.details: CookieConsentObservation Pydantic model (line 57) defines 10 structured fields for extraction. Scenario first checks for banner presence, scrolls if not found, then uses act_get() with CookieConsentObservation.model_json_schema() for structured data extraction. Post-interaction observations are re-extracted. Evidence includes screenshot_urls, button_labels, checkbox_states, text_snippets, and metadata with source='nova_act'. Mock audit events confirm findings structure with evidence data at /api/audits/{id}/findings endpoint.

VAL-NOVA-005.evidence: Code: _run_checkout_flow_scenario() (line 504) navigates to products, extracts CheckoutObservation with: page_reached, prices_seen, hidden_fees, price_delta, urgency_tactics, pre_selected_addons, required_steps. Persona-specific navigation (cost_sensitive seeks deals, privacy_sensitive avoids account creation, exit_intent browses hesitantly). Backend test: test_checkout_flow_returns_observation.
VAL-NOVA-005.details: CheckoutObservation Pydantic model (line 69) has 8 structured fields for price/fee extraction. Scenario navigates through checkout flow up to review page, extracting prices at product selection and final checkout stages. Price delta computed between initial and final prices. Evidence includes price_points, urgency language text_snippets, and checkbox_states for pre-selected add-ons.

VAL-NOVA-006.evidence: Code: _run_subscription_cancellation_scenario() (line 643) navigates to account/subscription management, extracts SubscriptionCancellationObservation with: cancellation_flow_found, clicks_to_cancel, detours_encountered, confirmshaming_detected/phrases, pause_offered, alternative_options, final_cancel_difficult. Backend test: test_cancellation_scenario_returns_observation.
VAL-NOVA-006.details: SubscriptionCancellationObservation model (line 81) has 8 fields covering obstruction patterns. Scenario searches for account settings, finds cancellation option, navigates through retention screens. Friction indicators auto-populated for confirmshaming, pause offers, and support detours. Evidence includes text_snippets of confirmshaming phrases and activity log of cancellation attempt steps.

VAL-NOVA-007.evidence: Code: _run_account_deletion_scenario() (line 793) navigates to privacy/account settings, extracts AccountDeletionObservation with: deletion_flow_found, clicks_to_delete, obstacles_encountered, requires_contact_support, confirmation_required, data_retention_warning, alternatives_offered, flow_completed. Backend test: test_deletion_scenario_returns_observation.
VAL-NOVA-007.details: AccountDeletionObservation model (line 95) has 8 fields covering deletion obstruction. Scenario searches settings, detects deletion option, navigates through confirmation steps. Friction indicators include 'Support contact required', 'Extra confirmation steps', and specific obstacles. Evidence includes obstacle descriptions, data retention warnings, and alternatives offered.

VAL-NOVA-008.evidence: Code: _run_newsletter_signup_scenario() (line 937) searches for signup forms, extracts NewsletterSignupObservation with: signup_form_found, pre_checked_boxes, confusing_opt_in, confusing_language_examples, dark_enrollment_detected, bundled_with_other_services, unsubscription_difficulty, consent_separated. Backend test: test_newsletter_scenario_returns_observation.
VAL-NOVA-008.details: NewsletterSignupObservation model (line 109) has 8 fields for dark enrollment detection. Scenario checks footer, checkout, account creation areas for signup elements. Privacy-sensitive persona attempts opt-out; exit_intent checks for exit-intent popups. Friction indicators auto-populated for pre-checked boxes, confusing language, and dark enrollment. Evidence includes checkbox_states for pre-checked options.

VAL-NOVA-009.evidence: Code: _run_pricing_comparison_scenario() (line 1067) browses for prices, extracts PricingComparisonObservation with: prices_found, price_variations_detected, bait_and_switch_suspected, persona_specific_offers, hidden_discounts, loyalty_program_pressure, dynamic_pricing_indicators. Price delta computed from multiple price points. Backend test: test_pricing_scenario_returns_observation.
VAL-NOVA-009.details: PricingComparisonObservation model (line 121) has 7 fields for sneaking detection. Scenario performs two-pass price check (initial browsing + checkout progression), comparing prices across stages. Evidence includes price_points list, dynamic pricing indicators text_snippets, and computed price_delta in metadata. Friction indicators for bait-and-switch and loyalty program pressure.

VAL-NOVA-010.evidence: Code: _capture_screenshot() method (line 1229) uses nova.page.screenshot() to capture PNG bytes, saves via storage provider with public_url and absolute_path. Called 20+ times across all 6 scenarios at key journey steps (initial, banner_found, after_interaction, product_selected, checkout_progress, etc.). Backend tests: test_screenshots_captured_during_scenario, test_screenshots_captured_at_key_steps. Mock audit events show screenshot URLs in details.image_url fields.
VAL-NOVA-010.details: Each scenario captures screenshots at: initial page load, scenario state found, after interaction. Cookie consent: initial, banner_found, after_interaction. Checkout: initial, product_selected, checkout_progress. Subscription: initial, account_area, cancel_flow. Account deletion: initial, settings_area, deletion_flow. Newsletter: initial, search_complete, interaction_complete. Pricing: initial, first_pass, second_pass. Seeded mock audit events confirm image_url presence (e.g., '/artifacts/screenshots/48fcd4cc.../cookie_consent_privacy_sensitive_1.svg').

VAL-NOVA-012.evidence: Code: _run_scenario_with_personas() (line 253) uses ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(self.max_workers, len(personas))) to run personas in parallel. Each persona dispatched to _run_single_persona() which calls scenario-specific method. Results stored as separate JourneyObservation objects with distinct persona field. run_audit() iterates all valid_scenarios × valid_personas combinations. Backend tests: test_parallel_persona_execution, test_multiple_personas_tested_for_same_url.
VAL-NOVA-012.details: ThreadPoolExecutor imported at line 12, used at line 264. max_workers defaults to 3 (constructor). Each scenario method has persona-specific behavior paths (e.g., privacy_sensitive vs cost_sensitive vs exit_intent navigation strategies). Seeded audit confirms multi-persona execution: events show cookie_consent/privacy_sensitive, cookie_consent/cost_sensitive, checkout_flow/privacy_sensitive, checkout_flow/cost_sensitive. PERSONA_DEFINITIONS in taxonomy.py defines 3 personas: privacy_sensitive, cost_sensitive, exit_intent.

frictions.description: The AuditCreateRequest schema uses field names 'scenarios' and 'personas', but the task prompt suggested using 'selected_scenarios' and 'selected_personas' in the POST body. Using the wrong field names causes the API to silently use default values instead of returning a validation error.
frictions.resolution: Identified the correct field names from reading the Pydantic schema (schemas/audit.py line 18-21). The API successfully accepts requests with the correct field names.

summary: Tested 9 assertions: all 9 passed. NovaActAuditProvider correctly implements all 6 scenario methods with structured Pydantic schema extraction via act_get(), screenshot capture at journey steps via nova.page.screenshot(), parallel persona execution via ThreadPoolExecutor, and proper integration with the provider registry for live mode selection. API endpoints return correct data structures (202 for creation, proper audit/findings schema). Backend test suite covers all tested behaviors.
